title: zeia glauca
intro: Zeia glauca, also known as Wild Indigo, is a perennial plant native to North America that belongs to the pea family Fabaceae. The medicinal properties of Zeia glauca lie in its leaves, which contain isoflavones and other bioactive compounds that have been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems. The health benefits of Zeia glauca include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, protect against cell damage, and prevent infections. Zeia glauca is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder for its medicinal uses, and is also used as a natural dye and in traditional folk medicine.
